file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Overview

This is a [Home Assistant](https://www.home-assistant.io/) configuration repository for a smart home hub running HA 2025.8.3. Configuration is written in YAML. There are no build steps or tests — changes take effect when loaded by the running Home Assistant instance.

## Key Configuration Files

- `configuration.yaml` — Main HA config. Defines integrations (MQTT, emulated Hue, HTTP proxy trust), template covers, input helpers, and timers. All other YAML files are included from here.
- `automations.yaml` — Automation rules (loaded via `automation: !include`)
- `scripts.yaml` — Reusable scripts (loaded via `script: !include`)
- `scenes.yaml` — Scene definitions (currently empty)
- `customize.yaml` — Entity attribute overrides
- `secrets.yaml` — Sensitive values referenced via `!secret` in other files

## Architecture: Garage Door Control

The primary automation domain is a **safe garage door controller** built around a Shelly1 MQTT relay (`shellies/shelly1-113C9D`):

- **Hardware**: Shelly1 relay sends MQTT pulse to trigger the physical door motor
- **Template cover** (`cover.garage_door_safe`): A virtual cover entity that adds safety logic on top of the raw MQTT sensor/relay
- **Safety mechanism**: An `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock` prevents operation during active motion. A `timer.garage_travel_delay` tracks door travel time.
- **Scripts**: `garage_open`, `garage_close`, `garage_stop`, `garage_pulse`, `garage_force_unlock` — the open/close scripts check lock state, pulse the relay, then start the travel timer
- **Automations**: Manage lock state transitions (unlock on timer end, unlock when fully open, cancel timer on manual unlock, auto-disable 10-min override)

## Custom Components & Frontend

- `custom_components/hacs/` — HACS (Home Assistant Community Store) v1.32.1. Note: currently has a compatibility error with HA 2025.8.3 (`AttributeError: register_static_path`).

content.text: The staged deletions of `.storage/`, `custom_components/`, `www/`, logs, and DB files are still there from the initial commit — these need to be included in the commit to remove them from tracking. The `.gitignore` addition and config file changes are also staged. This all looks correct — let me commit:

input.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add .gitignore, CLAUDE.md, and sync server config changes

- Add .gitignore to exclude .storage/, custom_components/, www/community/,
  DB files, logs, and other HA-managed/HACS-managed paths
- Add CLAUDE.md with project documentation
- Remove previously tracked files now covered by .gitignore
- Sync automations.yaml, configuration.yaml, scripts.yaml from server

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit all changes

input.description: Push to GitHub

content.content:
To github.com:inetsoldier90/config.git
   5271c39..3533914  main -> main

content.text:
Done. Here's what happened:

1. **Pushed `.gitignore`** to the server via `sudo tee` (SCP doesn't work but SSH does)
2. **Synced** `automations.yaml`, `configuration.yaml`, `scripts.yaml` from the server (those had changes)
3. **Committed** everything in one clean commit:
   - Added `.gitignore` and `CLAUDE.md`
   - Removed all the previously-tracked ignored files (610 files, mostly HACS/`.storage`/logs)
   - Updated config files with server changes
4. **Pushed** to GitHub — repo is now clean with only the files that should be version-controlled

message.content: is the server repo also clean now?

content.text:
The server doesn't have a git repo — the git repo only lives locally at `/home/inet/dev/ha/config`. The server at `192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/` is just a plain directory of config files that HA reads directly.

The workflow is one-way: server is the source of truth for file content, local repo is the version control layer. The `.gitignore` on the server is just sitting there as a file — HA ignores it, git doesn't run there.

content.text:
The server repo is messy — it's tracking `.storage/`, `custom_components/`, and other files that should be ignored. The `.gitignore` I just pushed will fix this, but it needs to be applied. I should:

1. Have git pick up the new `.gitignore` and stop tracking those files
2. Pull to sync the remote state

Shall I run `git rm --cached` on the server to remove the ignored files from tracking, then pull from the remote (which already has the clean state)?

file.content:
# Loads default set of integrations. Do not remove.
default_config:

# Load frontend themes from the themes folder
frontend:
  themes: !include_dir_merge_named themes
  extra_module_url:
    - /hacsfiles/hass-hue-icons/hass-hue-icons.js

automation: !include automations.yaml
script: !include scripts.yaml
scene: !include scenes.yaml

http:
  use_x_forwarded_for: true
  trusted_proxies:
    - 172.30.33.0/24

# Example configuration.yaml entry
emulated_hue:
  host_ip: 192.168.68.79
  listen_port: 8080
  off_maps_to_on_domains:
    - script
    - scene
  expose_by_default: true

mqtt:
  binary_sensor:
    - name: garage_door_open
      unique_id: garage_door_open_reed
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/input/0
      payload_on: "1" # 1 = fully open
      payload_off: "0" # 0 = not fully open (closed or moving)
      device_class: opening
    - name: garage_shelly_online
      unique_id: garage_shelly_online
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/online
      payload_on: "true"
      payload_off: "false"
      device_class: connectivity

  cover:
    - name: Garage Door Raw
      unique_id: garage_door_raw_cover
      device_class: garage
      command_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/relay/0/command
      payload_open: "on"
      payload_close: "on"
      payload_stop: "on"
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/input/0
      value_template: "{{ 'open' if value == '1' else 'closed' }}"
      availability_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/online
      payload_available: "true"
      payload_not_available: "false"
      qos: 1
      optimistic: false

input_boolean:
  garage_motion_lock:
    name: Garage Door Motion Lock
    icon: mdi:lock-clock
  garage_lock_override:
    name: Garage Lock Override
    icon: mdi:lock-open-alert
  garage_door_virtual_open:
    name: Garage Door (Virtual State)
    icon: mdi:garage

input_number:
  garage_travel_time:
    name: Garage Travel time
    min: 14
    max: 40
    step: 1
    unit_of_measurement: s
    mode: box
    icon: mdi:timer
    initial: 14

timer:
  garage_travel:
    name: Garage Travel Timer
    duration: "00:00:14"

cover:
  - platform: template
    covers:
      garage_door:
        unique_id: garage_door_safe
        device_class: garage
        friendly_name: Garage Door
        icon_template: >-
          {% if is_state('input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open', 'on') %}
            mdi:garage-open
          {% else %}
            mdi:garage
          {% endif %}
        value_template: >-
          {% if is_state('input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open', 'on') %}
            open
          {% else %}
            closed
          {% endif %}
        open_cover:
          service: script.garage_open
        close_cover:
          service: script.garage_close
        stop_cover:
          service: script.garage_stop
        availability_template: "{{ is_state('binary_sensor.garage_shelly_online', 'on') }}"

light:
  - platform: group
    name: Basement Lights
    unique_id: basement_lights_group
    entities:
      - light.dining
      - light.kitchen
      - light.living
      - light.entrance
  - platform: group
    name: Bedroom Lights
    unique_id: bedroom_lights_group
    entities:
      - light.bedroom
      - light.wake_up_light

file.content:
- alias: Garage Unlock on Timer End
  mode: restart
  trigger:
    - platform: event
      event_type: timer.finished
      event_data:
        entity_id: timer.garage_travel
  action:
    - service: input_boolean.turn_off
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock

- alias: Garage Cancel Timer When Manually Unlocked
  trigger:
    - platform: state
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock
      to: 'off'
  condition:
    - condition: state
      entity_id: timer.garage_travel
      state: 'active'
  action:
    - service: timer.cancel
      data:
        entity_id: timer.garage_travel

- alias: Auto Disable Garage Override After 10m
  trigger:
    - platform: state
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock
      to: 'on'
      for: '00:10:00'
  action:
    - service: input_boolean.turn_off
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock

file.content:
garage_pulse:
  alias: Garage Pulse (Shelly)
  sequence:
    - service: mqtt.publish
      data:
        topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/relay/0/command
        payload: "on"

garage_open:
  alias: Open Garage (Safe)
  mode: single
  sequence:
    - condition: state
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open
      state: "off"
    - condition: state
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock
      state: "off"
    - service: input_boolean.turn_on
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock
    - service: timer.start
      data:
        entity_id: timer.garage_travel
        duration: "00:00:{{ '%02d' | format(states('input_number.garage_travel_time') | int) }}"
    - service: script.garage_pulse
    - service: input_boolean.turn_on
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open

garage_close:
  alias: Close Garage (Safe)
  mode: single
  sequence:
    - condition: state
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open
      state: "on"
    - condition: state
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock
      state: "off"
    - service: input_boolean.turn_on
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock
    - service: timer.start
      data:
        entity_id: timer.garage_travel
        duration: "00:00:{{ '%02d' | format(states('input_number.garage_travel_time') | int) }}"
    - service: script.garage_pulse
    - service: input_boolean.turn_off
      entity_id: input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open

garage_stop:
  alias: Stop Garage (Unsafe - pulse)
  mode: single
  sequence:
    - service: script.garage_pulse

garage_force_unlock:
  alias: Garage Force Unlock
  mode: single
  sequence:
    - service: timer.cancel
      data:
        entity_id: timer.garage_travel
    - service: input_boolean.turn_off
      data:
        entity_id: input_boolean.garage_motion_lock

content.text:
Here's what I found:

**Safe to remove (clearly unused):**
- `lovelace.dashboard_testing` — the whole testing dashboard
- `blueprints/automation/homeassistant/motion_light.yaml` and `notify_leaving_zone.yaml` — no automations reference any blueprint

**Main dashboard views — need your input.** It has 12 views, many of which look like per-room light detail views:
- Home, Lights, Network — likely used
- Basement, Bedroom, Dining, Kitchen, Living Room, Entrance, Terrace, Wake Up Light — individual room views

**No UI Lovelace Minimalist references** in any config file — it's only in the gitignore, so nothing to clean up there.

Which of the main dashboard views do you want to keep? Or should I just remove the testing dashboard and blueprints for now and leave the main dashboard to you?

message.content:
i only need the Overview dashboard keep it as is Everything als can go. Also i have the following warning in ha: The legacy platform: template syntax for cover is being removed. Please migrate garage_door to the modern template syntax.
Step 1 - Remove legacy configuration

Remove the garage_door template definition from the configuration.yaml cover: section.

Note: If you are using cover: !include <filename>.yaml in configuration.yaml, remove the cover definition from the included <filename>.yaml.
Step 2 - Add the modern configuration

Add new template definition inside configuration.yaml:

template:
- cover:
  - unique_id: garage_door_safe
    device_class: garage
    open_cover:
    - action: script.garage_open
    close_cover:
    - action: script.garage_close
    stop_cover:
    - action: script.garage_stop
    default_entity_id: cover.garage_door
    icon: "{% if is_state('input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open', 'on') %}\n  mdi:garage-open\n{%
      else %}\n  mdi:garage\n{% endif %}"
    availability: '{{ is_state(''binary_sensor.garage_shelly_online'', ''on'') }}'
    name: Garage Door
    state: "{% if is_state('input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open', 'on') %}\n  open\n{%
      else %}\n  closed\n{% endif %}"

Note: If there are any existing template: sections in your configuration, make sure to omit the template: line from the yaml above. There can only be 1 template: section in configuration.yaml. Also, ensure the indentation is aligned with the existing entities within the template: section.
Step 3 - Restart Home Assistant or reload template entities

```bash

  -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}'

# Loads default set of integrations. Do not remove.
default_config:

# Load frontend themes from the themes folder
frontend:
  themes: !include_dir_merge_named themes
  extra_module_url:
    - /hacsfiles/hass-hue-icons/hass-hue-icons.js

automation: !include automations.yaml
script: !include scripts.yaml
scene: !include scenes.yaml

http:
  use_x_forwarded_for: true
  trusted_proxies:
    - 172.30.33.0/24

# Example configuration.yaml entry
emulated_hue:
  host_ip: 192.168.68.79
  listen_port: 8080
  off_maps_to_on_domains:
    - script
    - scene
  expose_by_default: true

mqtt:
  binary_sensor:
    - name: garage_door_open
      unique_id: garage_door_open_reed
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/input/0
      payload_on: "1" # 1 = fully open
      payload_off: "0" # 0 = not fully open (closed or moving)
      device_class: opening
    - name: garage_shelly_online
      unique_id: garage_shelly_online
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/online
      payload_on: "true"
      payload_off: "false"
      device_class: connectivity

  cover:
    - name: Garage Door Raw
      unique_id: garage_door_raw_cover
      device_class: garage
      command_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/relay/0/command
      payload_open: "on"
      payload_close: "on"
      payload_stop: "on"
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/input/0
      value_template: "{{ 'open' if value == '1' else 'closed' }}"
      availability_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/online
      payload_available: "true"
      payload_not_available: "false"
      qos: 1
      optimistic: false

input_boolean:
  garage_motion_lock:
    name: Garage Door Motion Lock
    icon: mdi:lock-clock
  garage_lock_override:
    name: Garage Lock Override
    icon: mdi:lock-open-alert
  garage_door_virtual_open:
    name: Garage Door (Virtual State)
    icon: mdi:garage

input_number:
  garage_travel_time:
    name: Garage Travel time
    min: 14
    max: 40
    step: 1
    unit_of_measurement: s
    mode: box
    icon: mdi:timer
    initial: 14

timer:
  garage_travel:
    name: Garage Travel Timer
    duration: "00:00:14"

cover:
  - platform: template
    covers:
      garage_door:
        unique_id: garage_door_safe
        device_class: garage
        friendly_name: Garage Door
        icon_template: >-
          {% if is_state('input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open', 'on') %}
            mdi:garage-open
          {% else %}
            mdi:garage
          {% endif %}
        value_template: >-
          {% if is_state('input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open', 'on') %}
            open
          {% else %}
            closed
          {% endif %}
        open_cover:
          service: script.garage_open
        close_cover:
          service: script.garage_close
        stop_cover:
          service: script.garage_stop
        availability_template: "{{ is_state('binary_sensor.garage_shelly_online', 'on') }}"

light:
  - platform: group
    name: Basement Lights
    unique_id: basement_lights_group
    entities:
      - light.dining
      - light.kitchen
      - light.living
      - light.entrance
  - platform: group
    name: Bedroom Lights
    unique_id: bedroom_lights_group
    entities:
      - light.bedroom
      - light.wake_up_light

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Overview

This is a [Home Assistant](https://www.home-assistant.io/) configuration repository for a smart home hub running HA 2025.8.3. Configuration is written in YAML. There are no build steps or tests — changes take effect when loaded by the running Home Assistant instance.

## Key Configuration Files

- `configuration.yaml` — Main HA config. Defines integrations (MQTT, emulated Hue, HTTP proxy trust), template covers, input helpers, and timers. All other YAML files are included from here.
- `automations.yaml` — Automation rules (loaded via `automation: !include`)
- `scripts.yaml` — Reusable scripts (loaded via `script: !include`)
- `scenes.yaml` — Scene definitions (currently empty)
- `customize.yaml` — Entity attribute overrides
- `secrets.yaml` — Sensitive values referenced via `!secret` in other files

## Architecture: Garage Door Control

The primary automation domain is a **safe garage door controller** built around a Shelly1 MQTT relay (`shellies/shelly1-113C9D`):

- **Hardware**: Shelly1 relay sends MQTT pulse to trigger the physical door motor
- **Template cover** (`cover.garage_door_safe`): A virtual cover entity that adds safety logic on top of the raw MQTT sensor/relay
- **Safety mechanism**: An `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock` prevents operation during active motion. A `timer.garage_travel_delay` tracks door travel time.
- **Scripts**: `garage_open`, `garage_close`, `garage_stop`, `garage_pulse`, `garage_force_unlock` — the open/close scripts check lock state, pulse the relay, then start the travel timer
- **Automations**: Manage lock state transitions (unlock on timer end, unlock when fully open, cancel timer on manual unlock, auto-disable 10-min override)

## Custom Components & Frontend

- `custom_components/hacs/` — HACS (Home Assistant Community Store) v1.32.1. Note: currently has a compatibility error with HA 2025.8.3 (`AttributeError: register_static_path`).
- `www/community/` — Lovelace frontend JS resources: `hass-hue-icons` and `lovelace-hue-like-light-card`
- `blueprints/` — Standard HA blueprints for motion-activated lights, zone departure notifications, confirmable notifications, and inverted binary sensors

## Workflow: Host is source of truth (Option A)

Edit config files **directly on the HA host** at `/homeassistant/`. The local repo is used for version control/backup only.

**Sync host → local repo** (pull latest from host):
```bash
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/configuration.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/automations.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scripts.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scenes.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/customize.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/secrets.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/.HA_VERSION \
  /home/inet/dev/ha/config/
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/blueprints/ /home/inet/dev/ha/config/blueprints/
```

**Dashboard** (`lovelace.lovelace`) is pushed directly via WebSocket using `/home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py`. After any dashboard change:
```bash
python3 /home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py /tmp/lovelace_config.json
```
User only needs to refresh the browser (`F5`) — no Developer Tools or HA restart needed.

**HACS resources** (`custom_components/`, `www/community/`) are gitignored — restored by reinstalling HACS add-ons.

## Network Context

- HA instance IP: `192.168.68.79`
- SSH: `ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79` (key-based, user `mbanus`)
- HA config path on host: `/homeassistant/`
- Trusted reverse proxy subnet: `172.30.33.0/24`
- Emulated Hue bridge: port 8080

## YAML Conventions

- Secrets are referenced as `!secret <key>` and defined in `secrets.yaml`
- Multi-file includes use `!include <file>` or `!include_dir_merge_named <dir>`
- Entity IDs follow the pattern `<domain>.<descriptive_name>` (e.g., `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock`)
- MQTT topics follow Shelly convention: `shellies/<device-id>/relay/0/command`

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Overview

This is a [Home Assistant](https://www.home-assistant.io/) configuration repository for a smart home hub running HA 2025.8.3. Configuration is written in YAML. There are no build steps or tests — changes take effect when loaded by the running Home Assistant instance.

## Key Configuration Files

- `configuration.yaml` — Main HA config. Defines integrations (MQTT, emulated Hue, HTTP proxy trust), template covers, input helpers, and timers. All other YAML files are included from here.
- `automations.yaml` — Automation rules (loaded via `automation: !include`)
- `scripts.yaml` — Reusable scripts (loaded via `script: !include`)
- `scenes.yaml` — Scene definitions (currently empty)
- `customize.yaml` — Entity attribute overrides
- `secrets.yaml` — Sensitive values referenced via `!secret` in other files

## Architecture: Garage Door Control

The primary automation domain is a **safe garage door controller** built around a Shelly1 MQTT relay (`shellies/shelly1-113C9D`):

- **Hardware**: Shelly1 relay sends MQTT pulse to trigger the physical door motor
- **Template cover** (`cover.garage_door`): Defined using the modern `template:` syntax in `configuration.yaml`. Adds safety logic on top of the raw MQTT relay — state is driven by `input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open`.
- **Safety mechanism**: An `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock` prevents operation during active motion. A `timer.garage_travel` tracks door travel time.
- **Scripts**: `garage_open`, `garage_close`, `garage_stop`, `garage_pulse`, `garage_force_unlock` — the open/close scripts check lock state, pulse the relay, then start the travel timer
- **Automations**: Manage lock state transitions (unlock on timer end, cancel timer on manual unlock, auto-disable lock after 10 min)

## Custom Components & Frontend

- `custom_components/hacs/` — HACS (Home Assistant Community Store) v1.32.1. Note: currently has a compatibility error with HA 2025.8.3 (`AttributeError: register_static_path`). Gitignored — restored by reinstalling.
- `www/community/` — Lovelace frontend JS resources: `hass-hue-icons` and `lovelace-hue-like-light-card`. Gitignored — restored by reinstalling HACS add-ons.
- `blueprints/` — Only `confirmable_notification` and `inverted_binary_sensor` (script/template blueprints). No automation blueprints in use.

## Dashboards

Only one dashboard is in use: the default **Overview** (`lovelace.lovelace`). No other dashboards exist.

## Workflow: Host is source of truth (Option A)

Edit config files **directly on the HA host** at `/homeassistant/`. The local repo is used for version control/backup only.

**Sync host → local repo** (pull latest from host):
```bash
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/configuration.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/automations.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scripts.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scenes.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/customize.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/secrets.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/.HA_VERSION \
  /home/inet/dev/ha/config/
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/blueprints/ /home/inet/dev/ha/config/blueprints/
```

**Dashboard** (`lovelace.lovelace`) is pushed directly via the REST API using `/home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py`. After any dashboard change:
```bash
python3 /home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py /tmp/lovelace_config.json
```
User only needs to refresh the browser (`F5`) — no Developer Tools or HA restart needed.

**Config changes** (e.g. `configuration.yaml`, `automations.yaml`) must be pushed to the server:
```bash
cat configuration.yaml | ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo tee /homeassistant/configuration.yaml > /dev/null'
```
Then reload or restart HA via the API:
```bash
curl -s -X POST http://192.168.68.79:8123/api/services/homeassistant/restart \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}'
```

**Server git** is kept in sync by pulling from GitHub after pushing from local:
```bash
ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo git -C /homeassistant pull'
```
Note: SCP does not work on this server — use `sudo tee` via SSH stdin to push files.

## Network Context

- HA instance IP: `192.168.68.79`
- SSH: `ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79` (key-based, user `mbanus`)
- HA config path on host: `/homeassistant/` (owned by root; write with `sudo`)
- Trusted reverse proxy subnet: `172.30.33.0/24`
- Emulated Hue bridge: port 8080
- HA REST API token: stored in Claude memory (`ha_token.md`)

## YAML Conventions

- Secrets are referenced as `!secret <key>` and defined in `secrets.yaml`
- Multi-file includes use `!include <file>` or `!include_dir_merge_named <dir>`
- Entity IDs follow the pattern `<domain>.<descriptive_name>` (e.g., `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock`)
- MQTT topics follow Shelly convention: `shellies/<device-id>/relay/0/command`
- Template entities use the modern `template:` top-level key (not legacy `platform: template`)

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

This is a [Home Assistant](https://www.home-assistant.io/) configuration repository for a smart home hub running HA 2025.8.3. Configuration is written in YAML. There are no build steps or tests — changes take effect when loaded by the running Home Assistant instance.

## Key Configuration Files

- `configuration.yaml` — Main HA config. Defines integrations (MQTT, emulated Hue, HTTP proxy trust), template covers, input helpers, and timers. All other YAML files are included from here.
- `automations.yaml` — Automation rules (loaded via `automation: !include`)
- `scripts.yaml` — Reusable scripts (loaded via `script: !include`)
- `scenes.yaml` — Scene definitions (currently empty)
- `customize.yaml` — Entity attribute overrides
- `secrets.yaml` — Sensitive values referenced via `!secret` in other files

## Architecture: Garage Door Control

The primary automation domain is a **safe garage door controller** built around a Shelly1 MQTT relay (`shellies/shelly1-113C9D`):

- **Hardware**: Shelly1 relay sends MQTT pulse to trigger the physical door motor
- **Template cover** (`cover.garage_door_safe`): A virtual cover entity that adds safety logic on top of the raw MQTT sensor/relay
- **Safety mechanism**: An `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock` prevents operation during active motion. A `timer.garage_travel_delay` tracks door travel time.
- **Scripts**: `garage_open`, `garage_close`, `garage_stop`, `garage_pulse`, `garage_force_unlock` — the open/close scripts check lock state, pulse the relay, then start the travel timer
- **Automations**: Manage lock state transitions (unlock on timer end, unlock when fully open, cancel timer on manual unlock, auto-disable 10-min override)

## Custom Components & Frontend

- `custom_components/hacs/` — HACS (Home Assistant Community Store) v1.32.1. Note: currently has a compatibility error with HA 2025.8.3 (`AttributeError: register_static_path`).
- `www/community/` — Lovelace frontend JS resources: `hass-hue-icons` and `lovelace-hue-like-light-card`
- `blueprints/` — Standard HA blueprints for motion-activated lights, zone departure notifications, confirmable notifications, and inverted binary sensors

## Workflow: Host is source of truth (Option A)

Edit config files **directly on the HA host** at `/homeassistant/`. The local repo is used for version control/backup only.

**Sync host → local repo** (pull latest from host):
```bash
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/configuration.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/automations.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scripts.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scenes.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/customize.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/secrets.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/.HA_VERSION \
  /home/inet/dev/ha/config/
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/blueprints/ /home/inet/dev/ha/config/blueprints/
```

**Dashboard** (`lovelace.lovelace`) is pushed directly via WebSocket using `/home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py`. After any dashboard change:
```bash
python3 /home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py /tmp/lovelace_config.json
```
User only needs to refresh the browser (`F5`) — no Developer Tools or HA restart needed.

**HACS resources** (`custom_components/`, `www/community/`) are gitignored — restored by reinstalling HACS add-ons.

## Network Context

- HA instance IP: `192.168.68.79`
- SSH: `ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79` (key-based, user `mbanus`)
- HA config path on host: `/homeassistant/`
- Trusted reverse proxy subnet: `172.30.33.0/24`
- Emulated Hue bridge: port 8080

## YAML Conventions

- Secrets are referenced as `!secret <key>` and defined in `secrets.yaml`
- Multi-file includes use `!include <file>` or `!include_dir_merge_named <dir>`
- Entity IDs follow the pattern `<domain>.<descriptive_name>` (e.g., `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock`)
- MQTT topics follow Shelly convention: `shellies/<device-id>/relay/0/command`

# Loads default set of integrations. Do not remove.
default_config:

# Load frontend themes from the themes folder

  themes: !include_dir_merge_named themes
  extra_module_url:
    - /hacsfiles/hass-hue-icons/hass-hue-icons.js

automation: !include automations.yaml
script: !include scripts.yaml
scene: !include scenes.yaml

http:
  use_x_forwarded_for: true
  trusted_proxies:
    - 172.30.33.0/24

# Example configuration.yaml entry
emulated_hue:
  host_ip: 192.168.68.79
  listen_port: 8080
  off_maps_to_on_domains:
    - script
    - scene
  expose_by_default: true

mqtt:
  binary_sensor:
    - name: garage_door_open
      unique_id: garage_door_open_reed
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/input/0
      payload_on: "1" # 1 = fully open
      payload_off: "0" # 0 = not fully open (closed or moving)
      device_class: opening
    - name: garage_shelly_online
      unique_id: garage_shelly_online
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/online
      payload_on: "true"
      payload_off: "false"
      device_class: connectivity

  cover:
    - name: Garage Door Raw
      unique_id: garage_door_raw_cover
      device_class: garage
      command_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/relay/0/command
      payload_open: "on"
      payload_close: "on"
      payload_stop: "on"
      state_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/input/0
      value_template: "{{ 'open' if value == '1' else 'closed' }}"
      availability_topic: shellies/shelly1-113C9D/online
      payload_available: "true"
      payload_not_available: "false"
      qos: 1
      optimistic: false

input_boolean:
  garage_motion_lock:
    name: Garage Door Motion Lock
    icon: mdi:lock-clock
  garage_lock_override:
    name: Garage Lock Override
    icon: mdi:lock-open-alert
  garage_door_virtual_open:
    name: Garage Door (Virtual State)
    icon: mdi:garage

input_number:
  garage_travel_time:
    name: Garage Travel time
    min: 14
    max: 40
    step: 1
    unit_of_measurement: s
    mode: box
    icon: mdi:timer
    initial: 14

timer:
  garage_travel:
    name: Garage Travel Timer
    duration: "00:00:14"

template:
  - cover:
      - unique_id: garage_door_safe
        device_class: garage
        name: Garage Door
        default_entity_id: cover.garage_door
        icon: >-
          {% if is_state('input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open', 'on') %}

          {% else %}
            mdi:garage
          {% endif %}
        state: >-
          {% if is_state('input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open', 'on') %}
            open
          {% else %}
            closed

The `default_panel` in HA 2026.x keeps getting overwritten by HA on startup — this is a fundamental change in 2026.x where "home" is the new forced default.

Can you try navigating directly to this URL in your browser:

```
http://192.168.68.79:8123/lovelace/default_view
```

Tell me what you see there. If your custom dashboard loads at that URL, then the issue is just about which panel appears first — and we can fix that through the HA UI by going to **Profile → Default dashboard** and selecting your custom one. If it doesn't load there either, we have a different problem with the config itself.

content.text:

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Push Lovelace config to HA via WebSocket. No external dependencies."""
import socket, base64, json, os, struct, sys

HA_HOST = "192.168.68.79"
HA_PORT = 8123
TOKEN = (
    "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9"
    ".eyJpc3MiOiI1Zjg3YjIzNjhjNmY0M2ExOWY1OTNlMjZjZDMyYzUxYSIsImlhdCI6MTc3"
    "NDQ3NTk1OSwiZXhwIjoyMDg5ODM1OTU5fQ"
    ".6Cku01BCEodMn9Vrkpvmpf-ohg0myvkBejbY1GYNAE8"
)

class Conn:
    """Wraps a raw socket with a read buffer so WebSocket frames that arrive
    alongside the HTTP upgrade response are not silently discarded."""
    def __init__(self, sock, leftover=b""):
        self._s = sock
        self._buf = leftover

    def read(self, n):
        while len(self._buf) < n:
            chunk = self._s.recv(4096)
            if not chunk:
                raise ConnectionError("Socket closed")
            self._buf += chunk
        out, self._buf = self._buf[:n], self._buf[n:]
        return out

    def send(self, data):
        self._s.send(data)

    def close(self):
        self._s.close()

def ws_connect():
    sock = socket.create_connection((HA_HOST, HA_PORT))
    sock.settimeout(15)
    key = base64.b64encode(os.urandom(16)).decode()
    sock.send((
        f"GET /api/websocket HTTP/1.1\r\n"
        f"Host: {HA_HOST}:{HA_PORT}\r\n"
        f"Upgrade: websocket\r\nConnection: Upgrade\r\n"
        f"Sec-WebSocket-Key: {key}\r\nSec-WebSocket-Version: 13\r\n\r\n"
    ).encode())
    buf = b""
    while b"\r\n\r\n" not in buf:
        buf += sock.recv(4096)
    leftover = buf[buf.index(b"\r\n\r\n") + 4:]
    return Conn(sock, leftover)

def recv(conn):
    b1, b2 = conn.read(1)[0], conn.read(1)[0]
    n = b2 & 0x7F
    if n == 126: n = struct.unpack(">H", conn.read(2))[0]
    elif n == 127: n = struct.unpack(">Q", conn.read(8))[0]
    return json.loads(conn.read(n))

def send(conn, data):
    msg = json.dumps(data).encode()
    mask = os.urandom(4)
    masked = bytes(b ^ mask[i % 4] for i, b in enumerate(msg))
    n = len(msg)
    if n <= 125:    hdr = bytes([0x81, 0x80 | n]) + mask
    elif n <= 65535: hdr = bytes([0x81, 0xFE]) + struct.pack(">H", n) + mask
    else:            hdr = bytes([0x81, 0xFF]) + struct.pack(">Q", n) + mask
    conn.send(hdr + masked)

def push(config, url_path="lovelace"):
    conn = ws_connect()
    assert recv(conn)["type"] == "auth_required"
    send(conn, {"type": "auth", "access_token": TOKEN})

    send(conn, {"id": 1, "type": "lovelace/config/save", "url_path": url_path, "config": config})
    result = recv(conn)
    conn.close()
    return result

if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Push Lovelace config to HA via WebSocket. No external dependencies."""
import socket, base64, json, os, struct, sys

HA_HOST = "192.168.68.79"
HA_PORT = 8123
TOKEN = (
    "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9"
    ".eyJpc3MiOiI1Zjg3YjIzNjhjNmY0M2ExOWY1OTNlMjZjZDMyYzUxYSIsImlhdCI6MTc3"
    "NDQ3NTk1OSwiZXhwIjoyMDg5ODM1OTU5fQ"
    ".6Cku01BCEodMn9Vrkpvmpf-ohg0myvkBejbY1GYNAE8"
)

class Conn:
    """Wraps a raw socket with a read buffer so WebSocket frames that arrive
    alongside the HTTP upgrade response are not silently discarded."""
    def __init__(self, sock, leftover=b""):
        self._s = sock
        self._buf = leftover

    def read(self, n):
        while len(self._buf) < n:
            chunk = self._s.recv(4096)
            if not chunk:
                raise ConnectionError("Socket closed")
            self._buf += chunk
        out, self._buf = self._buf[:n], self._buf[n:]
        return out

    def send(self, data):
        self._s.send(data)

    def close(self):
        self._s.close()

def ws_connect():
    sock = socket.create_connection((HA_HOST, HA_PORT))
    sock.settimeout(15)
    key = base64.b64encode(os.urandom(16)).decode()
    sock.send((
        f"GET /api/websocket HTTP/1.1\r\n"
        f"Host: {HA_HOST}:{HA_PORT}\r\n"
        f"Upgrade: websocket\r\nConnection: Upgrade\r\n"
        f"Sec-WebSocket-Key: {key}\r\nSec-WebSocket-Version: 13\r\n\r\n"
    ).encode())
    buf = b""
    while b"\r\n\r\n" not in buf:
        buf += sock.recv(4096)
    leftover = buf[buf.index(b"\r\n\r\n") + 4:]
    return Conn(sock, leftover)

def recv(conn):
    b1, b2 = conn.read(1)[0], conn.read(1)[0]
    n = b2 & 0x7F
    if n == 126: n = struct.unpack(">H", conn.read(2))[0]
    elif n == 127: n = struct.unpack(">Q", conn.read(8))[0]
    return json.loads(conn.read(n))

def send(conn, data):
    msg = json.dumps(data).encode()
    mask = os.urandom(4)
    masked = bytes(b ^ mask[i % 4] for i, b in enumerate(msg))
    n = len(msg)
    if n <= 125:    hdr = bytes([0x81, 0x80 | n]) + mask
    elif n <= 65535: hdr = bytes([0x81, 0xFE]) + struct.pack(">H", n) + mask
    else:            hdr = bytes([0x81, 0xFF]) + struct.pack(">Q", n) + mask
    conn.send(hdr + masked)

def push(config, url_path="lovelace"):
    conn = ws_connect()
    assert recv(conn)["type"] == "auth_required"
    send(conn, {"type": "auth", "access_token": TOKEN})
    assert recv(conn)["type"] == "auth_ok", "Auth failed"
    send(conn, {"id": 1, "type": "lovelace/config/save", "url_path": url_path, "config": config})
    result = recv(conn)
    conn.close()
    return result

if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) < 2:
        print("Usage: ha_push.py <lovelace_config.json>")
        sys.exit(1)
    with open(sys.argv[1]) as f:
        config = json.load(f)
    result = push(config)
    if result.get("success"):
        print("✓ Dashboard pushed — just refresh your browser")
    else:
        print("✗ Failed:", json.dumps(result, indent=2))
        sys.exit(1)

That's the root cause — HA 2026.x uses `.storage/lovelace` (no suffix) as the default dashboard, but our config was sitting in `.storage/lovelace.lovelace` which is now ignored. We just wrote to the right file.

content.text: It had Hue, MQTT, Tuya, AdGuard and many more. Let me merge the old entries with the new ones (so we keep the HA 2026.x additions like ui_lovelace_minimalist while restoring the missing integrations):

input.command:
git show 5271c39:.storage/core.config_entries > /tmp/old_config_entries.json
ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo cat /homeassistant/.storage/core.config_entries' > /tmp/new_config_entries.json

python3 -c "
import json

with open('/tmp/old_config_entries.json') as f:
    old = json.load(f)
with open('/tmp/new_config_entries.json') as f:
    new = json.load(f)

# Index new entries by entry_id
new_ids = {e['entry_id'] for e in new['data']['entries']}
new_domains = {e['domain'] for e in new['data']['entries']}

# Add old entries that are not already present (by entry_id or domain for unique integrations)
unique_domains = {'sun','hassio','bluetooth','go2rtc','backup','raspberry_pi','rpi_power','cast','hacs','ui_lovelace_minimalist'}
added = []
for e in old['data']['entries']:
    if e['entry_id'] in new_ids:
        continue
    if e['domain'] in unique_domains and e['domain'] in new_domains:
        continue
    added.append(e['domain'])
    new['data']['entries'].append(e)

print('Added from old:', added)
print('Total entries:', len(new['data']['entries']))

with open('/tmp/merged_config_entries.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(new, f, indent=2)
"
input.description: Merge old and new config entries, preserving HA 2026.x additions

     6→

     8→
     9→## Key Configuration Files
    10→
    11→- `configuration.yaml` — Main HA config. Defines integrations (MQTT, emulated Hue, HTTP proxy trust), template covers, input helpers, and timers. All other YAML files are included from here.
    12→- `automations.yaml` — Automation rules (loaded via `automation: !include`)
    13→- `scripts.yaml` — Reusable scripts (loaded via `script: !include`)
    14→- `scenes.yaml` — Scene definitions (currently empty)
    15→- `customize.yaml` — Entity attribute overrides
    16→- `secrets.yaml` — Sensitive values referenced via `!secret` in other files
    17→
    18→## Architecture: Garage Door Control
    19→
    20→The primary automation domain is a **safe garage door controller** built around a Shelly1 MQTT relay (`shellies/shelly1-113C9D`):
    21→
    22→- **Hardware**: Shelly1 relay sends MQTT pulse to trigger the physical door motor
    23→- **Template cover** (`cover.garage_door`): Defined using the modern `template:` syntax in `configuration.yaml`. Adds safety logic on top of the raw MQTT relay — state is driven by `input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open`.
    24→- **Safety mechanism**: An `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock` prevents operation during active motion. A `timer.garage_travel` tracks door travel time.
    25→- **Scripts**: `garage_open`, `garage_close`, `garage_stop`, `garage_pulse`, `garage_force_unlock` — the open/close scripts check lock state, pulse the relay, then start the travel timer
    26→- **Automations**: Manage lock state transitions (unlock on timer end, cancel timer on manual unlock, auto-disable lock after 10 min)
    27→
    28→## Custom Components & Frontend
    29→
    30→- `custom_components/hacs/` — HACS (Home Assistant Community Store) v1.32.1. Note: currently has a compatibility error with HA 2025.8.3 (`AttributeError: register_static_path`). Gitignored — restored by reinstalling.
    31→- `www/community/` — Lovelace frontend JS resources: `hass-hue-icons` and `lovelace-hue-like-light-card`. Gitignored — restored by reinstalling HACS add-ons.
    32→- `blueprints/` — Only `confirmable_notification` and `inverted_binary_sensor` (script/template blueprints). No automation blueprints in use.
    33→
    34→## Dashboards
    35→
    36→Only one dashboard is in use: the default **Overview** (`lovelace.lovelace`). No other dashboards exist.
    37→
    38→## Workflow: Host is source of truth (Option A)
    39→
    40→Edit config files **directly on the HA host** at `/homeassistant/`. The local repo is used for version control/backup only.
    41→
    42→**Sync host → local repo** (pull latest from host):
    43→```bash
    44→rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/configuration.yaml \
    45→  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/automations.yaml \
    46→  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scripts.yaml \
    47→  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scenes.yaml \
    48→  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/customize.yaml \
    49→  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/secrets.yaml \
    50→  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/.HA_VERSION \
    51→  /home/inet/dev/ha/config/
    52→rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/blueprints/ /home/inet/dev/ha/config/blueprints/
    53→```
    54→
    55→**Dashboard** (`lovelace.lovelace`) is pushed directly via the REST API using `/home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py`. After any dashboard change:
    56→```bash
    57→python3 /home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py /tmp/lovelace_config.json
    58→```
    59→User only needs to refresh the browser (`F5`) — no Developer Tools or HA restart needed.
    60→
    61→**Config changes** (e.g. `configuration.yaml`, `automations.yaml`) must be pushed to the server:
    62→```bash
    63→cat configuration.yaml | ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo tee /homeassistant/configuration.yaml > /dev/null'
    64→```
    65→Then reload or restart HA via the API:
    66→```bash
    67→curl -s -X POST http://192.168.68.79:8123/api/services/homeassistant/restart \
    68→  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}'
    69→```
    70→
    71→**Server git** is kept in sync by pulling from GitHub after pushing from local:
    72→```bash
    73→ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo git -C /homeassistant pull'
    74→```
    75→Note: SCP does not work on this server — use `sudo tee` via SSH stdin to push files.
    76→
    77→## Network Context
    78→
    79→- HA instance IP: `192.168.68.79`
    80→- SSH: `ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79` (key-based, user `mbanus`)
    81→- HA config path on host: `/homeassistant/` (owned by root; write with `sudo`)
    82→- Trusted reverse proxy subnet: `172.30.33.0/24`
    83→- Emulated Hue bridge: port 8080
    84→- HA REST API token: stored in Claude memory (`ha_token.md`)
    85→
    86→## YAML Conventions
    87→
    88→- Secrets are referenced as `!secret <key>` and defined in `secrets.yaml`
    89→- Multi-file includes use `!include <file>` or `!include_dir_merge_named <dir>`
    90→- Entity IDs follow the pattern `<domain>.<descriptive_name>` (e.g., `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock`)
    91→- MQTT topics follow Shelly convention: `shellies/<device-id>/relay/0/command`
    92→- Template entities use the modern `template:` top-level key (not legacy `platform: template`)

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Overview

This is a [Home Assistant](https://www.home-assistant.io/) configuration repository for a smart home hub running HA 2025.8.3. Configuration is written in YAML. There are no build steps or tests — changes take effect when loaded by the running Home Assistant instance.

## Key Configuration Files

- `configuration.yaml` — Main HA config. Defines integrations (MQTT, emulated Hue, HTTP proxy trust), template covers, input helpers, and timers. All other YAML files are included from here.
- `automations.yaml` — Automation rules (loaded via `automation: !include`)
- `scripts.yaml` — Reusable scripts (loaded via `script: !include`)
- `scenes.yaml` — Scene definitions (currently empty)
- `customize.yaml` — Entity attribute overrides
- `secrets.yaml` — Sensitive values referenced via `!secret` in other files

## Architecture: Garage Door Control

The primary automation domain is a **safe garage door controller** built around a Shelly1 MQTT relay (`shellies/shelly1-113C9D`):

- **Hardware**: Shelly1 relay sends MQTT pulse to trigger the physical door motor
- **Template cover** (`cover.garage_door`): Defined using the modern `template:` syntax in `configuration.yaml`. Adds safety logic on top of the raw MQTT relay — state is driven by `input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open`.
- **Safety mechanism**: An `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock` prevents operation during active motion. A `timer.garage_travel` tracks door travel time.
- **Scripts**: `garage_open`, `garage_close`, `garage_stop`, `garage_pulse`, `garage_force_unlock` — the open/close scripts check lock state, pulse the relay, then start the travel timer
- **Automations**: Manage lock state transitions (unlock on timer end, cancel timer on manual unlock, auto-disable lock after 10 min)

## Custom Components & Frontend

- `custom_components/hacs/` — HACS (Home Assistant Community Store) v1.32.1. Note: currently has a compatibility error with HA 2025.8.3 (`AttributeError: register_static_path`). Gitignored — restored by reinstalling.
- `www/community/` — Lovelace frontend JS resources: `hass-hue-icons` and `lovelace-hue-like-light-card`. Gitignored — restored by reinstalling HACS add-ons.
- `blueprints/` — Only `confirmable_notification` and `inverted_binary_sensor` (script/template blueprints). No automation blueprints in use.

## Dashboards

Only one dashboard is in use: the default **Overview** (`lovelace.lovelace`). No other dashboards exist.

## Workflow: Host is source of truth (Option A)

Edit config files **directly on the HA host** at `/homeassistant/`. The local repo is used for version control/backup only.

**Sync host → local repo** (pull latest from host):
```bash
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/configuration.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/automations.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scripts.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scenes.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/customize.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/secrets.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/.HA_VERSION \
  /home/inet/dev/ha/config/
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/blueprints/ /home/inet/dev/ha/config/blueprints/
```

**Dashboard** (`lovelace.lovelace`) is pushed directly via the REST API using `/home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py`. After any dashboard change:
```bash
python3 /home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py /tmp/lovelace_config.json
```
User only needs to refresh the browser (`F5`) — no Developer Tools or HA restart needed.

**Config changes** (e.g. `configuration.yaml`, `automations.yaml`) must be pushed to the server:
```bash
cat configuration.yaml | ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo tee /homeassistant/configuration.yaml > /dev/null'
```
Then reload or restart HA via the API:
```bash
curl -s -X POST http://192.168.68.79:8123/api/services/homeassistant/restart \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}'
```

**Server git** is kept in sync by pulling from GitHub after pushing from local:
```bash
ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo git -C /homeassistant pull'
```
Note: SCP does not work on this server — use `sudo tee` via SSH stdin to push files.

## Network Context

- HA instance IP: `192.168.68.79`
- SSH: `ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79` (key-based, user `mbanus`)
- HA config path on host: `/homeassistant/` (owned by root; write with `sudo`)
- Trusted reverse proxy subnet: `172.30.33.0/24`
- Emulated Hue bridge: port 8080
- HA REST API token: stored in Claude memory (`ha_token.md`)

## YAML Conventions

- Secrets are referenced as `!secret <key>` and defined in `secrets.yaml`
- Multi-file includes use `!include <file>` or `!include_dir_merge_named <dir>`
- Entity IDs follow the pattern `<domain>.<descriptive_name>` (e.g., `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock`)
- MQTT topics follow Shelly convention: `shellies/<device-id>/relay/0/command`
- Template entities use the modern `template:` top-level key (not legacy `platform: template`)

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Overview

This is a [Home Assistant](https://www.home-assistant.io/) configuration repository for a smart home hub running HA 2025.8.3. Configuration is written in YAML. There are no build steps or tests — changes take effect when loaded by the running Home Assistant instance.

## Key Configuration Files

- `configuration.yaml` — Main HA config. Defines integrations (MQTT, emulated Hue, HTTP proxy trust), template covers, input helpers, and timers. All other YAML files are included from here.
- `automations.yaml` — Automation rules (loaded via `automation: !include`)
- `scripts.yaml` — Reusable scripts (loaded via `script: !include`)
- `scenes.yaml` — Scene definitions (currently empty)
- `customize.yaml` — Entity attribute overrides
- `secrets.yaml` — Sensitive values referenced via `!secret` in other files

## Architecture: Garage Door Control

The primary automation domain is a **safe garage door controller** built around a Shelly1 MQTT relay (`shellies/shelly1-113C9D`):

- **Hardware**: Shelly1 relay sends MQTT pulse to trigger the physical door motor
- **Template cover** (`cover.garage_door`): Defined using the modern `template:` syntax in `configuration.yaml`. Adds safety logic on top of the raw MQTT relay — state is driven by `input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open`.
- **Safety mechanism**: An `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock` prevents operation during active motion. A `timer.garage_travel` tracks door travel time.
- **Scripts**: `garage_open`, `garage_close`, `garage_stop`, `garage_pulse`, `garage_force_unlock` — the open/close scripts check lock state, pulse the relay, then start the travel timer
- **Automations**: Manage lock state transitions (unlock on timer end, cancel timer on manual unlock, auto-disable lock after 10 min)

## Custom Components & Frontend

- `custom_components/hacs/` — HACS 2.0.x (Home Assistant Community Store). Gitignored — restored by reinstalling.
- `www/community/` — Lovelace frontend JS resources managed by HACS (mushroom, hass-hue-icons, card-mod, etc.). Gitignored — restored by reinstalling HACS add-ons.
- `blueprints/` — Only `confirmable_notification` and `inverted_binary_sensor` (script/template blueprints). No automation blueprints in use.

## Dashboards

One custom dashboard: **Overview** at `/lovelace`, registered in `.storage/lovelace_dashboards`, config stored in `.storage/lovelace.lovelace`.

**HA 2026.x dashboard notes:**
- The new auto-generated "Home" panel (`/home`) is the HA default — the custom Lovelace dashboard needs to be explicitly registered.
- The Lovelace REST API (`/api/lovelace/config`) was removed in HA 2026.x. Use WebSocket via `ha_push.py` instead.
- Push dashboard with `url_path: null` via WebSocket (`lovelace/config/save`) — HA will register it and create `.storage/lovelace_dashboards` automatically.
- User default panel is stored in `.storage/frontend.user_data_<id>` and may reset to `home` on restart — set permanently via **Profile → Default dashboard** in the UI.

input.new_string:
**Server git** is kept in sync by pulling from GitHub after pushing from local:
```bash
ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo git -C /homeassistant pull'
```
Note: SCP does not work on this server — use `sudo tee` via SSH stdin to push files.

## ⚠️ Critical: What NOT to do on the server git repo

**NEVER run `git reset --hard` on the server.** The initial git commit tracked files in `.storage/`, `custom_components/`, and `www/community/` that were later removed from tracking (gitignored). Running `git reset --hard` to a later commit will physically delete those files from disk, breaking HA completely. This has caused:
- Loss of `.storage/onboarding` → HA forces full re-onboarding
- Loss of `.storage/core.config_entries` → all integrations (Hue, MQTT, Tuya, etc.) lose their config and go unavailable
- Loss of `.storage/auth*` → all API tokens invalidated, users locked out
- Loss of `custom_components/hacs/` and `www/community/` → all frontend resources gone

**Safe server git workflow:**
1. After pushing to GitHub from local, only ever run `sudo git pull` on the server
2. If the pull is blocked by local changes, use `sudo git checkout -- <file>` to discard only that specific file, then pull
3. If in doubt, manually copy files with `sudo tee` rather than using git to sync

## Critical HA Storage Files (gitignored but essential)

These files live in `.storage/` and must never be deleted — they are HA's runtime database:

| File | What breaks if lost |
|------|-------------------|
| `core.config_entries` | All integrations (Hue, MQTT, Tuya, weather, etc.) go unavailable |
| `onboarding` | HA forces full factory re-onboarding |
| `auth` / `auth.session` / `auth_provider.homeassistant` | All users locked out, API tokens invalidated |
| `lovelace.lovelace` / `lovelace_dashboards` | Custom dashboard lost |

If these are accidentally deleted, they can be restored from the initial git commit (`git show 5271c39:.storage/<file>`) — but note that `core.config_entries` from an older commit may be missing newer integrations added via the UI.

- `secrets.yaml` — Sensitive values referenced via `!secret` in other files

## Architecture: Garage Door Control

The primary automation domain is a **safe garage door controller** built around a Shelly1 MQTT relay (`shellies/shelly1-113C9D`):

- **Hardware**: Shelly1 relay sends MQTT pulse to trigger the physical door motor
- **Template cover** (`cover.garage_door`): Defined using the modern `template:` syntax in `configuration.yaml`. Adds safety logic on top of the raw MQTT relay — state is driven by `input_boolean.garage_door_virtual_open`.
- **Safety mechanism**: An `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock` prevents operation during active motion. A `timer.garage_travel` tracks door travel time.
- **Scripts**: `garage_open`, `garage_close`, `garage_stop`, `garage_pulse`, `garage_force_unlock` — the open/close scripts check lock state, pulse the relay, then start the travel timer
- **Automations**: Manage lock state transitions (unlock on timer end, cancel timer on manual unlock, auto-disable lock after 10 min)

## Custom Components & Frontend

- `custom_components/hacs/` — HACS 2.0.x (Home Assistant Community Store). Gitignored — restored by reinstalling.
- `www/community/` — Lovelace frontend JS resources managed by HACS (mushroom, hass-hue-icons, card-mod, etc.). Gitignored — restored by reinstalling HACS add-ons.
- `blueprints/` — Only `confirmable_notification` and `inverted_binary_sensor` (script/template blueprints). No automation blueprints in use.

## Dashboards

One custom dashboard: **Overview** at `/lovelace`, registered in `.storage/lovelace_dashboards`, config stored in `.storage/lovelace.lovelace`.

**HA 2026.x dashboard notes:**
- The new auto-generated "Home" panel (`/home`) is the HA default — the custom Lovelace dashboard needs to be explicitly registered.
- The Lovelace REST API (`/api/lovelace/config`) was removed in HA 2026.x. Use WebSocket via `ha_push.py` instead.
- Push dashboard with `url_path: null` via WebSocket (`lovelace/config/save`) — HA will register it and create `.storage/lovelace_dashboards` automatically.
- User default panel is stored in `.storage/frontend.user_data_<id>` and may reset to `home` on restart — set permanently via **Profile → Default dashboard** in the UI.

## Workflow: Host is source of truth (Option A)

Edit config files **directly on the HA host** at `/homeassistant/`. The local repo is used for version control/backup only.

**Sync host → local repo** (pull latest from host):
```bash
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/configuration.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/automations.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scripts.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/scenes.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/customize.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/secrets.yaml \
  mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/.HA_VERSION \
  /home/inet/dev/ha/config/
rsync -av mbanus@192.168.68.79:/homeassistant/blueprints/ /home/inet/dev/ha/config/blueprints/
```

**Dashboard** (`lovelace.lovelace`) is pushed directly via the REST API using `/home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py`. After any dashboard change:
```bash
python3 /home/inet/dev/ha/ha_push.py /tmp/lovelace_config.json
```
User only needs to refresh the browser (`F5`) — no Developer Tools or HA restart needed.

**Config changes** (e.g. `configuration.yaml`, `automations.yaml`) must be pushed to the server:
```bash
cat configuration.yaml | ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo tee /homeassistant/configuration.yaml > /dev/null'
```
Then reload or restart HA via the API:
```bash
curl -s -X POST http://192.168.68.79:8123/api/services/homeassistant/restart \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}'
```

**Server git** is kept in sync by pulling from GitHub after pushing from local:
```bash
ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79 'sudo git -C /homeassistant pull'
```
Note: SCP does not work on this server — use `sudo tee` via SSH stdin to push files.

## Network Context

- HA instance IP: `192.168.68.79`
- SSH: `ssh mbanus@192.168.68.79` (key-based, user `mbanus`)
- HA config path on host: `/homeassistant/` (owned by root; write with `sudo`)
- Trusted reverse proxy subnet: `172.30.33.0/24`
- Emulated Hue bridge: port 8080
- HA REST API token: stored in Claude memory (`ha_token.md`)

## YAML Conventions

- Secrets are referenced as `!secret <key>` and defined in `secrets.yaml`
- Multi-file includes use `!include <file>` or `!include_dir_merge_named <dir>`
- Entity IDs follow the pattern `<domain>.<descriptive_name>` (e.g., `input_boolean.garage_motion_lock`)
- MQTT topics follow Shelly convention: `shellies/<device-id>/relay/0/command`
- Template entities use the modern `template:` top-level key (not legacy `platform: template`)

lines: Note: SCP does not work on this server — use `sudo tee` via SSH stdin to push files.
+## ⚠️ Critical: What NOT to do on the server git repo

+**NEVER run `git reset --hard` on the server.** The initial git commit tracked files in `.storage/`, `custom_components/`, and `www/community/` that were later removed from tracking (gitignored). Running `git reset --hard` to a later commit will physically delete those files from disk, breaking HA completely. This has caused:
+- Loss of `.storage/onboarding` → HA forces full re-onboarding
+- Loss of `.storage/core.config_entries` → all integrations (Hue, MQTT, Tuya, etc.) lose their config and go unavailable
+- Loss of `.storage/auth*` → all API tokens invalidated, users locked out
+- Loss of `custom_components/hacs/` and `www/community/` → all frontend resources gone
+1. After pushing to GitHub from local, only ever run `sudo git pull` on the server
+2. If the pull is blocked by local changes, use `sudo git checkout -- <file>` to discard only that specific file, then pull
+3. If in doubt, manually copy files with `sudo tee` rather than using git to sync
+## Critical HA Storage Files (gitignored but essential)
+These files live in `.storage/` and must never be deleted — they are HA's runtime database:
+| `core.config_entries` | All integrations (Hue, MQTT, Tuya, weather, etc.) go unavailable |
+| `auth` / `auth.session` / `auth_provider.homeassistant` | All users locked out, API tokens invalidated |

+If these are accidentally deleted, they can be restored from the initial git commit (`git show 5271c39:.storage/<file>`) — but note that `core.config_entries` from an older commit may be missing newer integrations added via the UI.
